message:
Integrate toolchain detection into hook transfer pipeline

Completes the end-to-end toolchain synchronization flow by detecting
the project's toolchain and passing it to the remote worker. This
is part of the Automatic Toolchain Synchronization epic
(remote_compilation_helper-ayn).

Hook Integration (rch/src/hook.rs):
- Call detect_toolchain() for project root before remote execution
- Pass detected ToolchainInfo to execute_remote_streaming()
- Graceful degradation: if detection fails, logs warning and continues
- Worker receives toolchain info and can install/wrap as needed

Detection Flow:
1. Check rust-toolchain.toml in project root
2. Fall back to legacy rust-toolchain file
3. Fall back to rustc --version output
4. On any failure, proceed without toolchain specification

Config Integration (rch/src/config.rs):
- Added circuit field to config merge logic
- Ensures CircuitBreakerConfig is properly propagated from overlays
- Supports per-project circuit breaker customization

Logging:
- Debug log when toolchain is successfully detected
- Warning log when detection fails (with error details)
- Info log for command execution with toolchain context

This enables the full toolchain sync flow:
  Agent -> Hook (detect) -> Daemon (select) -> Worker (ensure/wrap)

The worker will now receive the exact toolchain specification and
ensure it's available before executing compilation commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
